title: 11 Herbal Tinctures For Itchy Scalp
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for an itchy scalp are a great natural remedy to try. So, why do they work? Well, when you apply these tinctures to your scalp, the active ingredients in the herbs help to soothe and calm the skin. This is especially true for herbs like Calendula officinalis, which is known for its anti-inflammatory properties. It can help to reduce redness and itching, giving you relief from that annoying itch.  Another herb that's great for an itchy scalp is Urtica dioica, also known as nettle leaf. It has antihistamine properties that can help to reduce inflammation and itching. This makes it a great natural remedy for conditions like eczema and psoriasis.  Aloe barbadensis, also known as aloe vera, is another popular herb for skin issues. It's got anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm the skin. When applied to the scalp, it can help to reduce itching and redness, giving you a more comfortable scalp.  Using herbal tinctures for an itchy scalp has several benefits. For one, they're a natural remedy, so you don't have to worry about harsh chemicals or side effects. They're also relatively inexpensive compared to prescription medications, and they're easy to use – just apply the tincture to your scalp and massage it in. Additionally, many herbal tinctures are multi-purpose, so you can use them to treat other skin issues or even internal health problems. This makes them a great addition to your natural health routine.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis tinctures contains bioactive constituents like triterpene saponins and flavonoids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help soothe and calm an itchy scalp. The triterpene saponins in calendula tincture can reduce inflammation and irritation, while the flavonoids have antioxidant properties that protect the scalp from damage. The antiseptic properties of calendula help prevent infections that can cause itchiness and flakiness. By reducing inflammation, preventing infections, and protecting the scalp, calendula officinalis tinctures can promote a healthy and comfortable scalp.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal tinctures for itchy scalp
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Calendula officinalis flowers and 1 cup of 80% vodka or glycerin in a clean glass container.
Combine Calendula flowers and vodka/glycerin in the container. Make sure the flowers are completely covered.
Store the container in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container. Discard the solids.
Transfer the tincture to a dropper bottle and use 5-7 drops, mixed with a tablespoon of coconut oil or olive oil, to apply to the itchy scalp.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica tinctures contains bioactive constituents like histamine, acetylcholine, and beta-sitosterol. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antipruritic properties, which help reduce inflammation and itching on the scalp. The antihistamine properties of histamine and acetylcholine in Urtica dioica tinctures can block the release of histamine, a chemical that causes itching. Beta-sitosterol, a sterol found in the plant, has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and calm the skin. By reducing inflammation and blocking the release of histamine, Urtica dioica tinctures can help alleviate itchy scalp conditions.
remedies.image_alt: Urtica dioica herbal tinctures for itchy scalp
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Urtica dioica leaves, stems, and flowers. Clean and chop them.
Combine 1 cup of chopped Urtica dioica with 2 cups of 80% vodka in a glass jar.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another glass jar. Discard the solids.
Bottle the tincture in dark glass bottles and label them. Store them in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tinctures contains bioactive constituents like aloin, aloe-emodin, and acemannan. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help soothe and calm an itchy scalp. Aloin and aloe-emodin reduce inflammation and prevent further irritation, while acemannan stimulates the immune system to fight off fungal and bacterial infections that can cause itchiness. The anti-inflammatory properties of aloe barbadensis tinctures also help to reduce redness and swelling on the scalp, providing quick relief from itchiness. By targeting the root causes of itchy scalp, aloe barbadensis tinctures provide a natural and effective solution to this common problem.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal tinctures for itchy scalp
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Aloe barbadensis gel, 2 cups of vodka, and a clean glass jar.
Combine the Aloe gel and vodka in the glass jar, and stir well.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar.
Store the Aloe tincture in the fridge and use 10-15 drops as a scalp treatment, 2-3 times a week.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum tinctures contains bioactive constituents like flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help soothe an itchy scalp. The sesquiterpene lactones in Silybum marianum tinctures, specifically silymarin, have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote skin health. This can lead to a decrease in itchiness and discomfort on the scalp. The flavonoids in Silybum marianum tinctures have antioxidant properties, which help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals.
remedies.image_alt: Silybum marianum herbal tinctures for itchy scalp
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Silybum marianum leaves and flowers. Clean and dry the area where you will be working.
Combine the Silybum marianum with 2 cups of 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar. Make sure the herbs are completely covered by the vodka.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks. Shake the jar daily to help the herbs infuse into the vodka.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Label the jar with the date and the contents. Store the tincture in a cool, dark place. Use 5-10 drops of the tincture on the itchy scalp and gently massage it in.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum tinctures contains bioactive constituents like hyperforin and hypericin. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help soothe and calm an itchy scalp. The tannins in Hypericum perforatum also have astringent properties, reducing the production of sebum and oil that can clog pores and irritate the scalp. Hypericum perforatum tinctures also contain flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that help protect the scalp from damage and promote healthy skin. By reducing inflammation, sebum production, and promoting healthy skin, Hypericum perforatum tinctures can help alleviate an itchy scalp and promote a healthy scalp environment.
remedies.image_alt: Hypericum perforatum herbal tinctures for itchy scalp
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Hypericum perforatum flowers and 2 cups of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Add the Hypericum perforatum flowers to the vodka, making sure they are completely covered.
Close the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in a cool, dark place and use 5-7 drops on itchy scalp 2-3 times a day as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia tinctures contains compounds like cineole and terpinen-4-ol. These bioactive constituents have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe itchy scalp. Cineole reduces inflammation, while terpinen-4-ol kills bacteria and fungi that can cause irritation. This tincture's antifungal properties also help control scalp conditions like dandruff and seborrheic dermatitis. By reducing inflammation and killing bacteria, Melaleuca alternifolia tinctures can calm itchy scalp and promote healthy scalp skin.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal tinctures for itchy scalp
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Melaleuca alternifolia leaves and flowers, 2 cups of 80% vodka, and a glass jar.
Combine the leaves and flowers in the glass jar, and cover them with the vodka.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another glass jar, discarding the solids.
Label the jar and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 5-7 drops of the tincture in a carrier oil for an itchy scalp treatment.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa tinctures contains active constituents like avenacosides and avenanthramides. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and redness on the scalp. Avenanthramides also have antioxidant properties, which can protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of avenacosides and avenanthramides can help soothe and calm an itchy scalp, reducing irritation and discomfort. By reducing inflammation and protecting the scalp, Avena sativa tinctures can provide relief from itchy scalp conditions.
remedies.image_alt: Avena sativa herbal tinctures for itchy scalp
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Avena sativa dried root, 2 cups of vodka, and a glass jar with a lid.
Combine the Avena sativa root and vodka in the glass jar, making sure the plant material is completely covered.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture to a dropper bottle and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 5-10 drops as needed to soothe an itchy scalp.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera tinctures contains bioactive compounds like aloin, aloe-emodin, and acemannan that help soothe an itchy scalp. Aloin has anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling on the scalp. Aloe-emodin has antibacterial properties, killing bacteria that can cause irritation and itchiness. Acemannan helps calm the scalp by reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy environment for skin cells to grow. These properties in aloe vera tinctures work together to provide relief from an itchy scalp.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe vera herbal tinctures for itchy scalp
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Aloe vera gel and a clean glass jar.
Combine 2 tablespoons of vodka or witch hazel with the Aloe vera gel in the glass jar.
Stir the mixture well and cover the jar with a lid.
Let the mixture sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass jar. Store the tincture in the fridge.

remedies.remedy_desc: Equisetum arvense tinctures contains flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins. These bioactive constituents have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe itchy scalps. The saponins in Equisetum arvense have a cleansing effect, removing dirt and other irritants that can cause itchiness. The flavonoids and phenolic acids have antioxidant properties, which can help calm and balance the scalp's natural flora. By reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy scalp environment, Equisetum arvense tinctures can help alleviate itchy scalp issues.
remedies.image_alt: Equisetum arvense herbal tinctures for itchy scalp
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Equisetum arvense roots and stems, dried.
Combine 1 cup of dried Equisetum arvense with 2 cups of 80% vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it daily.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar.
Label the tincture and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 10-20 drops as needed for an itchy scalp.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis tinctures contains rosmarinic acid, luteolin, and melissic acid, which are powerful antioxidants. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling on the scalp. Rosmarinic acid in particular has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, which can contribute to itchiness. Melissa officinalis tinctures also contains flavonoids that have a soothing effect on the skin, calming irritation and itchiness. By reducing inflammation and soothing the skin, Melissa officinalis tinctures can help alleviate itchy scalp issues.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal tinctures for itchy scalp
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Melissa officinalis dried leaves and flowers.
Combine the dried Melissa officinalis with 2 cups of 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in a cool, dark place and use 10-20 drops as needed to soothe an itchy scalp.

remedies.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale tinctures contains bioactive constituents like flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and alkaloids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness and swelling on the scalp. Taraxacum officinale tinctures also contains antioxidants like quercetin, which protect the scalp from free radicals and promote healthy skin cells. The antifungal and antibacterial properties of sesquiterpenes and alkaloids in Taraxacum officinale tinctures help control scalp infections and soothe itchiness. By reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin, Taraxacum officinale tinctures can help alleviate itchy scalp conditions.
remedies.image_alt: Taraxacum officinale herbal tinctures for itchy scalp
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers. Clean them with water.
Combine the cleaned Taraxacum officinale with 2 cups of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar. Stir well.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks. Shake the jar every day.
After 2 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Store the tincture in a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid. Use 20-30 drops as needed to soothe itchy scalp.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help soothe an itchy scalp. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and chamomile, have anti-inflammatory properties that can calm irritation and reduce itchiness. These teas work by relaxing the scalp and reducing stress, which can contribute to an itchy scalp. This might bring some relief from discomfort.
faq_safe: Consuming herbal teas for an itchy scalp daily is generally safe, but be cautious of potential interactions. Some teas, like peppermint or chamomile, can be too potent if drunk excessively. It's also possible to develop an allergic reaction or sensitivity to certain herbs. Monitor your body's response and adjust your consumption accordingly.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas for an itchy scalp can take a few days to a week to show some results. As the herbal ingredients work to soothe and calm the scalp, you might notice a decrease in itchiness and irritation. Some people see improvements within 24 to 48 hours, but it really depends on the individual and the specific herbal tea used.
faq_time: For an itchy scalp, it's best to drink herbal tea in the morning or early afternoon. This allows the tea's soothing properties to take effect before the day gets busy. The active ingredients can help calm irritation and reduce inflammation, making it a great way to start your day or take a break from a long morning.
faq_interaction: Some herbal teas may interact with prescription medications for an itchy scalp. For example, St. John's Wort can affect how your body processes certain medications, making them stronger or weaker than they should be. This can lead to side effects or make the medication less effective. Be cautious when combining herbal teas with prescription medications.
faq_pregnant: When you're pregnant, it's a good idea to be careful with herbal teas. Some teas might contain ingredients that could harm your baby. If you have an itchy scalp, you might consider using coconut oil or olive oil instead of herbal teas. These oils can help soothe itchy skin and are generally safe to use during pregnancy.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas for itchy scalp can be a good option while breastfeeding. Many herbs like peppermint, chamomile, and calendula are safe and gentle. However, it's essential to check the ingredients and choose a tea that's free from additives and artificial flavors. Look for teas specifically labeled as "breastfeeding safe" or made from organic herbs.
